name: Chinese Stuffed Peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Remove the tops from the peppers and scoop out the seeds, placing them in a 9-inch square baking dish.
3. In a medium saucepan of boiling salted water, cook the rice for 7 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water twice.
4. Heat oil in a medium skillet. Add scallion, ginger, and garlic, cooking over medium-high heat until fragrant but not browned (about 1 minute). Transfer to a medium bowl.
5. In the same bowl, combine ground pork, peas, rice, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon sherry, 1/4 teaspoon sugar, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and salt. Mix gently to avoid crushing the peas.
6. Stuff the peppers with the meat mixture.
7. In a 2-cup glass measure, combine beef broth, remaining sherry, soy sauce, sugar, and pepper. Pour the sauce over the stuffed peppers and cover with foil.
8. Bake for 45 minutes. Remove the foil and reduce the oven temperature to 350°F. Continue baking, basting a few times with sauce, for an additional 15 minutes.
9. Transfer peppers to a serving platter. Place the baking dish on top of the stove. Dissolve cornstarch in 2 tablespoons water and whisk into the liquid in the baking dish. Cook over medium heat until the sauce boils and thickens (about 2 minutes). Serve sauce with peppers.
